1000.name: Device type
1000.description:
* bit 16-31: Additional information
* bit 0-15: Device profile number

1001.name: Error register
1001.description:
* bit 7: manufacturer specific
* bit 6: Reserved (always 0)
* bit 5: device profile specific
* bit 4: communication error (overrun, error state)
* bit 3: temperature
* bit 2: voltage
* bit 1: current
* bit 0: generic error

1003.name: Pre-defined error field
1003.description:
* Sub Index 0: Contains number of actual errors. 0 can be written to clear error history.
* sub-index 1 and above:
  * bit 16-31: Manufacturer specific additional information
  * bit 0-15: Error code as transmited in the Emergency object

1005.name: COB-ID SYNC message
1005.description:
* bit 31: set to 0
* bit 30: If set, CANopen device generates SYNC object
* bit 11-29: set to 0
* bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

1006.name: Communication cycle period
1006.description: Period of SYNC transmission in µs (0 = transmission disabled).

1007.name: Synchronous window length
1007.description: Synchronous window leghth in µs (0 = not used). All synchronous PDOs must be transmitted within this time window.

1010.name: Store parameters
1010.description:
Sub-indexes 1 and above:
* Reading provides information about its storage functionality:
  * bit 1: If set, CANopen device saves parameters autonomously
  * bit 0: If set, CANopen device saves parameters on command
* Writing value 0x65766173 ('s','a','v','e' from LSB to MSB) stores corresponding data.

1011.name: Restore default parameters
1011.description:
Sub-indexes 1 and above:
* Reading provides information about its restoring capability:
  * bit 0: If set, CANopen device restores parameters
* Writing value 0x64616F6C ('l','o','a','d' from LSB to MSB) restores corresponding data.

1012.name: COB-ID time stamp object
1012.description:
* bit 31: If set, CANopen device consumes TIME message
* bit 30: If set, CANopen device produces TIME message
* bit 11-29: set to 0
* bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

1014.name: COB-ID EMCY
1014.description:
* bit 31: If set, EMCY does NOT exist / is NOT valid
* bit 11-30: set to 0
* bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

1015.name: Inhibit time EMCY
1015.description: Inhibit time of emergency message in multiples of 100µs. The value 0 disables the inhibit time.

1016.name: Consumer heartbeat time
1016.description:
Consumer Heartbeat Time:
  * bit 24-31: set to 0
  * bit 16-23: Node ID of the monitored node. If 0 or greater than 127, sub-entry is not used.
  * bit 0-15: Heartbeat time in ms (if 0, sub-intry is not used). Value should be higher than the corresponding producer heartbeat time.

1017.name: Producer heartbeat time
1017.description: Heartbeat producer time in ms (0 = disable transmission).

1018.name: Identity
1018.description:
* Vendor-ID, assigned by CiA
* Product code, manufacturer specific
* Revision number:
  * bit 16-31: Major revision number (CANopen behavior has changed)
  * bit 0-15: Minor revision num. (CANopen behavior has not changed)
* Serial number, manufacturer specific

1019.name: Synchronous counter overflow value
1019.description:
* Value 0: SYNC message is transmitted with data length 0.
* Value 1: reserved.
* Value 2-240: SYNC message has one data byte, which contains the counter.
* Value 241-255: reserved.

1028.name: Emergency consumer object
1028.description:
Emergency consumer 1 to 127:
* bit 31: If set, EMCY consumer does NOT exist / is NOT valid
* bit 11-30: set to 0
* bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

1029.name: Error behavior object
1029.description:
Sub-indexes 1 and above:
* Value 0x00: on error change to NMT state Pre-operational (only if currently in NMT state Operational)
* Value 0x01: on error do nothing
* Value 0x02: on error change to NMT state Stopped

1200.name: SDO server parameter
1200.description:
Sub-indexes 1 and 2:
* bit 11-31: set to 0
* bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

1201.name: SDO server parameter
1201.description:
* Sub-indexes 1 and 2:
  * bit 31: If set, SDO does NOT exist / is NOT valid
  * bit 30: If set, value is assigned dynamically
  * bit 11-29: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID
* Node-ID of the SDO client, 0x01 to 0x7F

1280.name: SDO client parameter
1280.description:
* Sub-indexes 1 and 2:
  * bit 31: If set, SDO does NOT exist / is NOT valid
  * bit 30: If set, value is assigned dynamically
  * bit 11-29: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID
* Node-ID of the SDO server, 0x01 to 0x7F

1400.name: RPDO communication parameter
1400.description:
* COB-ID used by RPDO:
  * bit 31: If set, PDO does not exist / is not valid
  * bit 11-30: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID
* Transmission type:
  * Value 0-240: synchronous, processed after next reception of SYNC object
  * Value 241-253: not used
  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Event timer in ms (0 = disabled) for deadline monitoring.

1401.name: RPDO communication parameter
1401.description:
* COB-ID used by RPDO:
  * bit 31: If set, PDO does not exist / is not valid
  * bit 11-30: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID
* Transmission type:
  * Value 0-240: synchronous, processed after next reception of SYNC object
  * Value 241-253: not used
  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Event timer in ms (0 = disabled) for deadline monitoring.

1402.name: RPDO communication parameter
1402.description:
* COB-ID used by RPDO:

  * bit 11-30: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID

  * Value 241-253: not used
  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Event timer in ms (0 = disabled) for deadline monitoring.

1403.name: RPDO communication parameter
1403.description:

  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Event timer in ms (0 = disabled) for deadline monitoring.

1404.name: RPDO communication parameter
1404.description:
* COB-ID used by RPDO:
  * bit 31: If set, PDO does not exist / is not valid

  * Value 241-253: not used
  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Event timer in ms (0 = disabled) for deadline monitoring.

1600.name: RPDO mapping parameter
1600.description:
* Number of mapped application objects in PDO:
  * Value 0: mapping is disabled.
  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:
  * bit 16-31: index
  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits

00.name: Number of mapped application objects in PDO

1601.name: RPDO mapping parameter
1601.description:
* Number of mapped application objects in PDO:
  * Value 0: mapping is disabled.
  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:
  * bit 16-31: index
  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits

00.name: Number of mapped application objects in PDO

1602.name: RPDO mapping parameter
1602.description:

  * Value 0: mapping is disabled.
  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:
  * bit 16-31: index
  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits

00.name: Number of mapped application objects in PDO

1603.name: RPDO mapping parameter
1603.description:

  * Value 0: mapping is disabled.
  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:
  * bit 16-31: index
  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits

1604.name: RPDO mapping parameter
1604.description:

  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:

  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits

00.name: Number of mapped application objects in PDO

1800.name: TPDO communication parameter
1800.description:
* COB-ID used by RPDO:
  * bit 31: If set, PDO does not exist / is not valid
  * bit 30: If set, NO RTR is allowed on this PDO
  * bit 11-29: set to 0
  * bit 0-10: 11-bit CAN-ID
* Transmission type:
  * Value 0: synchronous (acyclic)
  * Value 1-240: synchronous (cyclic every (1-240)-th sync)
  * Value 241-253: not used
  * Value 254: event-driven (manufacturer-specific)
  * Value 255: event-driven (device profile and application profile specific)
* Inhibit time in multiple of 100µs, if the transmission type is set to 254 or 255 (0 = disabled).
* Event timer interval in ms, if the transmission type is set to 254 or 255 (0 = disabled).
* SYNC start value
  * Value 0: Counter of the SYNC message shall not be processed.
  * Value 1-240: The SYNC message with the counter value equal to this value shall be regarded as the first received SYNC message.

1801.name: TPDO communication parameter
1801.description:

  * bit 31: If set, PDO does not exist / is not valid

1A00.name: TPDO mapping parameter
1A00.description:
* Number of mapped application objects in PDO:
  * Value 0: mapping is disabled.
  * Value 1: sub-index 0x01 is valid.
  * Value 2-8: sub-indexes 0x01 to (0x02 to 0x08) are valid.
* Application object 1-8:
  * bit 16-31: index
  * bit 8-15: sub-index
  * bit 0-7: data length in bits
